selection_guidance: Prefer one discrete feature per session.
If you harden an existing behavior, add or tighten automated tests and mark the item `verified`.
Update this file and `claude-progress.txt` before closing a session.

status_legend.verified: Implemented and backed by tests or documented validation evidence.
status_legend.implemented: Implemented in code/docs but missing direct coverage or stronger evidence.
status_legend.awaiting_validation: Adapter path exists, but real hardware or manual evidence is still missing.

features.title: Root project spec exists for long-running sessions
features.description: The repo keeps a concise root-level spec describing goals, invariants, non-goals, and session workflow.
verification: Review app_spec.txt for public contract, validation commands, and session rules.

features.title: Feature catalog exists as JSON
features.description: The repo keeps a machine-readable feature inventory with statuses, evidence, and verification commands.

features.title: Bootstrap script validates the repo for agent sessions
features.description: A root-level script refreshes the editable install, runs import smoke checks, and executes the baseline test suite.

features.title: Session progress log is persisted in the repo
features.description: A root-level progress log captures completed sessions, open issues, and the expected workflow.
verification: Review claude-progress.txt for checklist, open issues, and dated entries.

features.title: Existing agent playbook references the harness files
features.description: Contributor guidance points agents to the root harness files and session loop.

features.title: Core package installs without mandatory vendor dependencies
features.description: Base `omnismi` installs without `pynvml` or `amdsmi` so CI and CPU-only users can integrate the API.

features.title: NVIDIA extra installs NVML bindings
features.description: The `nvidia` extra exposes `nvidia-ml-py` for NVIDIA environments.

features.title: AMD extra installs AMD SMI bindings
features.description: The `amd` extra exposes `amdsmi` for ROCm environments.

features.title: Combined extra installs both vendor backends
features.description: The `all` extra installs both `nvidia-ml-py` and `amdsmi` for mixed environments.

features.title: Public API exports stay minimal and avoid `get_*` names
features.description: The top-level contract is centered on `count`, `gpus`, and `gpu` with no legacy getter aliases.

features.title: count() returns the number of visible GPUs
features.description: The top-level count reflects the number of GPUs surfaced through active backends.

features.title: gpus() returns globally indexed GPU objects
features.description: Devices returned from all active backends are wrapped as `GPU` objects with stable global indexes.

features.title: gpu(index) returns a device or None
features.description: Negative or out-of-range indexes return `None` instead of raising.

features.title: GPU.info() returns GPUInfo
features.description: Static device info is exposed as the frozen public `GPUInfo` dataclass.

features.title: GPU.info() degrades gracefully on backend errors
features.description: If backend info collection fails, Omnismi returns a partial `GPUInfo` instead of surfacing vendor exceptions.
verification: Inspect exception handling in GPU.info() and add direct tests if behavior changes.

features.title: GPU.metrics() returns GPUMetrics
features.description: Dynamic device metrics are exposed as the frozen public `GPUMetrics` dataclass.

features.title: GPU.metrics() degrades gracefully on backend errors
features.description: If backend metric collection fails, Omnismi returns a partial metrics object with nullable fields and a fresh timestamp.
verification: Inspect exception handling in GPU.metrics() and add direct tests if behavior changes.

features.title: GPU.realtime() falls back to a no-op context manager when unsupported
features.description: Backends that do not implement realtime mode should not break user code.
verification: Inspect GPU.realtime() fallback behavior in src/omnismi/api.py.

features.title: Public models remain frozen slotted dataclasses
features.description: The public data contract uses immutable slotted dataclasses for predictable shape and low overhead.

features.title: Registry keeps NVIDIA and AMD as default backends
features.description: The default backend factory list initializes NVIDIA first and AMD second.

features.title: active_backends() filters unavailable backends
features.description: Unavailable backends are ignored instead of causing user-visible errors.

features.title: close_all() closes instantiated backends and resets the cache
features.description: Backend shutdown is centralized so long-lived processes can reset adapter state safely.

features.title: normalize_text() treats common NA strings as missing values
features.description: String, bytes, and wrapped values normalize cleanly and collapse known NA sentinels to `None`.
verification: Inspect normalize_text(), normalize_uuid(), and helper functions in src/omnismi/normalize.py.

features.title: normalize_bytes() rejects invalid or negative memory values
features.description: Memory values normalize to integer bytes or `None` when invalid.

features.title: normalize_percent() accepts percentage fractions
features.description: Values between 0 and 1 normalize to percentage points when appropriate.

features.title: normalize_temperature_c() supports millicelsius inputs
features.description: Temperature values normalize to Celsius and reject implausible readings.

features.title: normalize_power_w() converts common vendor units to Watts
features.description: Power readings normalize from W, mW, or uW and reject implausible results.

features.title: normalize_clock_mhz() converts Hz and kHz inputs to MHz
features.description: Clock readings normalize to MHz with sanity bounds.

features.title: NVIDIA backend imports and initializes NVML lazily
features.description: NVML is only imported and initialized when the NVIDIA backend is actually used.

features.title: NVIDIA backend enumerates devices via NVML handles
features.description: Device discovery returns `(vendor_index, handle)` tuples for each visible NVIDIA GPU.

features.title: NVIDIA backend exposes normalized static info
features.description: The backend reads name, UUID, driver version, and total memory from NVML.

features.title: NVIDIA backend exposes normalized dynamic metrics
features.description: The backend reads utilization, memory, temperature, power, and clocks from NVML.

features.title: NVIDIA metrics default to cached psutil-style sampling
features.description: Repeated metric reads reuse cached values until the sampler refreshes them.

features.title: NVIDIA realtime mode bypasses the metrics cache
features.description: Inside `GPU.realtime()` / `backend.realtime_mode()`, each metric read hits NVML directly.

features.title: NVIDIA backend shutdown stops sampling and closes NVML
features.description: Closing the backend stops the background sampler, clears cache state, and calls `nvmlShutdown()`.

features.title: AMD backend imports and initializes AMD SMI lazily
features.description: AMD SMI is imported and initialized only when the AMD backend is used.

features.title: AMD backend supports both processor-handle and gpu-handle discovery
features.description: Handle lookup falls back across AMD SMI API variants for broader compatibility.

features.title: AMD backend exposes normalized static info
features.description: The backend reads market name, serial/UUID-like identifier, driver information, and total VRAM.

features.title: AMD backend falls back across EDGE, HOTSPOT, VRAM, and legacy temperature APIs
features.description: Temperature reads prefer modern AMD SMI sensors and fall back when a sensor is missing.

features.title: AMD backend falls back across power API variants
features.description: Power reads use socket power first, then legacy GPU power paths when needed.

features.title: AMD backend falls back across clock API variants
features.description: Core and memory clocks are resolved through modern clock info APIs with legacy fallback support.

features.title: AMD backend exposes normalized dynamic metrics
features.description: The backend reads utilization, memory, temperature, power, and clocks from AMD SMI.

features.title: AMD backend shutdown supports multiple AMD SMI close methods
features.description: Closing the backend works with both `amdsmi_shut_down()` and `amdsmi_shutdown()` variants.

features.title: No-backend hosts return empty results instead of errors
features.description: CPU-only or unconfigured hosts should still be able to import and call the API safely.

features.title: Parity CLI accepts vendor, sample count, and interval arguments
features.description: The parity command provides a stable CLI contract for local vendor comparison runs.

features.title: Parity command compares Omnismi against direct NVIDIA readings
features.description: NVIDIA parity evaluation gathers Omnismi samples, collects direct NVML samples, and applies tolerances.

features.title: Parity command compares Omnismi against direct AMD readings
features.description: AMD parity evaluation gathers Omnismi samples, collects direct AMD SMI samples, and applies tolerances.

features.title: README stays short while detailed docs live under docs/
features.description: The repo keeps the README usage-oriented and defers deeper references to the docs site content.

features.title: Compatibility matrix tracks verified and pending hardware evidence
features.description: Compatibility docs distinguish verified hardware from adapter paths that still need user validation.

features.title: Roadmap documents near-term and long-term platform work
features.description: The roadmap keeps v1.x follow-up items and longer-term adapter strategy visible.

features.title: Docs are configured for MkDocs builds
features.description: The repo includes MkDocs configuration and docs dependencies for local site builds.

features.title: Validate NVIDIA H100 on real hardware
features.description: Promote H100 support from adapter-path evidence to verified compatibility with parity output and environment details.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate NVIDIA H200 on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for H200 systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate NVIDIA B200 on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for B200 systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate NVIDIA RTX 4090 on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for desktop-class NVIDIA systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate AMD MI250 on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for MI250 systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate AMD MI300A on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for MI300A systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate AMD MI325X on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for MI325X systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Validate AMD RX 7900 XTX on real hardware
features.description: Collect parity evidence and environment metadata for desktop-class AMD systems.
verification: Update docs/compatibility.md and CHANGELOG.md with evidence.

features.title: Add an Intel adapter without changing the public API
features.description: Future Intel support should fit under the existing `count/gpus/gpu` and `info/metrics` contract.
verification: Add backend implementation, tests, docs, and compatibility entries.

features.title: Add an Apple/Metal adapter without changing the public API
features.description: Future Apple support should fit under the existing `count/gpus/gpu` and `info/metrics` contract.
verification: Add backend implementation, tests, docs, and compatibility entries.

features.title: Improve backend diagnostics and debug visibility
features.description: Add more explicit diagnostics for import failures, partial metric availability, and environment drift without weakening graceful defaults.
verification: Add tests/docs for the new diagnostics surface.

features.title: Review scheduler/runtime integration helpers after v1.x stabilizes
features.description: Only revisit framework or scheduler-specific helpers after the core observability contract remains stable.
verification: Update app_spec, roadmap, docs, and tests before expanding the API.
